titleStmt.title: SII 31.205: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 205. (A.R. No. B 209 of 1938.) CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the inside of the west prākāra wall in the Agastyēśvarasvāmi temple.
It is dated in Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Āśvija, śu. 6, corresponding to September 13, Wednesday, 1553 A.D.

Registers an agreement arrived at between Pedamalōju, Chinamallōju, sons of Agasāla Komḍōju, Basvaya, the son of Papana and Pāpōju son of Chadipirēla papoju, Siddhayya, son of Yaṟalingōju to share their rights and incomes (kāṇichi and ōjarika) at the ratio of three-fourth (3/4) and one-fourth (1/4) respectively derived from elevan villages viz., Kamalāpuram, Chaḍi(piṭ)rālu, Tuṁmaluru etc.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti . śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1475 Aghu(gu)nēṭi pramādīca saṁvatsara Āśvi(śvī)ja śudda 6 lu śrīmatu kāmalāpuraṁ A
gasāla koṁḍōju koḍukulu pedamallōju cinamallōju pāpanakoḍuku basvaya I jana 3 nnu tamakomāḷlu cadipirēla pāpoju koḍuku pāpōju
ku yaṟaliṁgōju koḍuku śiddayyakuṁnnu yiccina grāmāla pāḷladhaṁma kramameṭlaṁnnanu kāmalāpuraṁ cadipirāla tuṁmalūru yaraguḍipāḍu tippalūru
pāsugulapāḍu jaṁmāpuraṁ paṁdiḷla palleṁ gollapalle Aṁmireḍḍi palle Agastē(sta)yīpadakoṁḍu kāṇāduluṁnnu vōjarikāluṁnu manamu
paṁcukoṁnna vivaraṁ duṁmmalūri bhūmāju koḍuku pāpōjuṁnu cadipirēla yarabayirōju koḍuku koṁḍōjunnu vīri yiddarikiṁnni yīgrāmālalōnu
mūḍōrapōṁgānu migilina reṁḍu pāḷlaṁnu mīru muṁnnu cerusagamunnu naḍapaṁgalavāraṁ yiṭlani voppiyiccina yiṁdkusākṣulu goṁgaḷreḍḍiṁnni ceṁnā
reṁḍinni pedireḍḍi liṁgareḍḍinni Anaṁttareḍḍiṁnni karutula nāgābattuṁḍu vaḍlarābattu vīrala vubhayānikinni karaṇaṁ raghunāthayya komāruṁḍu
tiṁmmayyavrāśina gāmapāḷaka yaraṁmma yiccina pedamallōjuvrālu . cinamallōjuvrālu . sākṣivrālu goṁgaḷreḍḍivrālu rācamareḍḍivālu ceṁnāreḍḍivrālu .
Digital edition of SII 31.205 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
